name: Salad from the Orient
recipeInstructions:
- Use olive oil or a mixture of olive and peanut oil.
1. Cut the pork into very thin slices, suitable for stir-frying. Mix brown sugar, 1-1/2 teaspoons soy sauce, sherry (optional), and Hoisin sauce. Pour over the pork strips in a heavy plastic bag. Tie the bag and place it in the refrigerator to marinate for up to 1 hour, turning the bag occasionally.
2. To prepare the dressing, combine oil, sugar, 2 tablespoons soy sauce, grated ginger root, and Tabasco or other hot sauce. Blend and place in the refrigerator to chill.
3. Cook vermicelli to desired doneness. Add pea pods and carrots during the last 2 minutes of cooking. Drain and rinse immediately with cold water. Place into a large bowl. Pour the dressing over the vermicelli mixture, add the green onions (slice into thin rounds, tops included). Set aside while preparing the pork.
4. Remove the pork from the refrigerator and place it in a wok or large heavy skillet. Include some of the marinade. Stir fry the pork strips until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Drain and add to the salad mixture. Mix well. Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour to allow flavors to blend. If desired, sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds just before serving.
